title: Search Inside Logs for Errors
scenario: A web application has been misbehaving. Its logs are stored across multiple files in /var/log/webapp/. You need to hunt down errors and warnings using grep, count their occurrences, and save your findings.
symptoms: Logs scattered across multiple files in /var/log/webapp/
mission: Use grep to search for errors and warnings in the webapp logs and save your findings to the appropriate output files.

hints.title: Case-Sensitive Search
hints.content: Case-sensitive search: Use `grep 'ERROR' /var/log/webapp/error.log` to find exact matches.

hints.title: Counting Matches
hints.content: Counting matches: Use `grep -c 'WARNING' /var/log/webapp/error.log` to count matching lines and save the number with >.

hints.title: Recursive Search
hints.content: Recursive search: Use `grep -ri 'error' /var/log/webapp/` to search all files in a directory at once.

hints.title: Bonus: Line Numbers
hints.content: Bonus: Add -n to any grep command to include line numbers in your output.

acceptance_criteria.description: /home/intern/errors.txt exists and contains ERROR lines from error.log
/home/intern/warning_count.txt exists and contains a number greater than 0
